channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ex-Abercrombie CEO has dementia and is unfit for sex trafficking trial, prosecutors and defense say
item.description: Prosecutors and defense lawyers say the former longtime CEO of Abercrombie & Fitch is suffering from dementia and isn't competent to stand trial on federal sex trafficking charges in New York.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mayor: Family was to celebrate child's birthday when NYC sightseeing helicopter crashed, killing 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A family from Spain was about to celebrate the ninth birthday of one of their children when their sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey, killing all six people aboard, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, DAVE COLLINS and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Doctors remove pig kidney from an Alabama woman after a record 130 days
item.description: An Alabama woman who lived with a pig kidney for a record 130 days had the organ removed and is back on dialysis.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Greenland space base commander removed after Vance criticism
item.description: The Trump administration removed the Greenland space base commander Thursday after discovering the leader sent out an email breaking stride with the vice president.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Texas families impacted by SNAP cuts; food banks brace for direct hit
item.description: LEANDER, Texas (KXAN) — Chari Seeley happily looks at a list of items. She points to the cabbage and cheese. A few seconds later she shouts, "whipping cream!" It's not something she would usually get but she explained it's for a special occasion. "It's my birthday," she sang, laughing.
item.dc:creator: David Barer, NewsNation

item.title: Plane crash near Florida highway kills 3 on board, injures 1 on the ground
item.description: A spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said the plane, a Cessna 310, crashed at around 10:20 a.m. after departing from the nearby airport.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KREX 5

item.title: US Attorney Habba investigating New Jersey governor over immigration enforcement policy
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — The top federal prosecutor in New Jersey says she has launched an investigation into Democratic Gov. Phil Murphy and state Attorney General Matt Platkin over the state's directive to local law enforcement not to cooperate with federal agent conducting immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Murder defendant Karen Read says she would've 'cheered' OJ Simpson verdict as she prepares for 2nd trial
item.description: Karen Read says she would've "cheered" the OJ Simpson verdict if it happened today, in a reversal from her original reaction to his double murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 American women arrested at border for smuggling unaccompanied minors: CBP
item.description: Two U.S. citizens have been arrested for smuggling unaccompanied Mexican girls into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Judge will decide whether to proceed with Menendez brothers resentencing hearing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge is set to decide Friday whether to proceed with resentencing hearings for Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of murdering their parents, in light of Los Angeles' new district attorney opposing their release after 30 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Olivia Munn mocks Trump's tariffs during surprise 'Daily Show' appearance
item.description: "Trump knows exactly what he's doing. He put tariffs that destroyed the global economy, so then he took them off, and now it's only mostly destroyed," Munn said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: New York bills would protect Tesla owners, target Tesla dominance
item.description: That measure follows nationwide reports of Tesla owners, dealerships, and charging stations being vandalized.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: US stocks dive as euphoria on Wall Street reverts to fear about US-China trade war
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks dove Thursday and surrendered a chunk of their historic gains from the day before as President Donald Trump’s trade war continues to threaten the economy.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Helicopter crashes in New York City's Hudson River; 6 dead
item.description: A Bell 206 helicopter belonging to a tour company crashed in the water near Jersey City and Hoboken around 3:15 p.m., according to NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Universal Studios announces plans to build first European theme park resort
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences officially announced on Tuesday that it intends to build its first-ever theme park and resort in the United Kingdom. In 2023, Universal purchased a 476-acre parcel of land outside Bedford, England, about 55 miles north of London.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 124 dead after roof collapse at Dominican club, officials scramble to ID victims
item.description: Rescue crews from Puerto Rico and Israel arrived Wednesday to help with the search. Officials said crews used sonar to detect what could be one person still breathing under the rubble.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Check out these artists at the Red Butte Garden 2025 Outdoor Concert Series
item.description: Red Butte Garden has officially announced which artists will take the stage during its Outdoor Concert Series this year.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Didn't get a Trader Joe's mini pastel tote? You can buy one for over $1,000 on eBay
item.description: And as they did last year, customers were quick to snag the bags. If you weren't among them, you're in luck — but only if you're willing to spend a pretty penny.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: A pipeline company filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners. Now its project is threatened
item.description: MANSFIELD, S.D. (AP) — Jared Bossly was planting soybeans one spring night in 2023 on his 2,000-acre farm in South Dakota when he spotted a sheriff’s vehicle parked at the corner of his property. He had a hunch it wasn’t a social visit.
item.dc:creator: BY ERIC FERKENHOFF/LEE ENTERPRISES and JOSH KELETY/AP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Group calls for investigation into Ms. Rachel over posts about Gaza children
item.description: Rachel Griffin Accurso, who goes by Ms. Rachel to her more than 14 million subscribers on YouTube, makes videos for children.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Salt Lake Bees' new stadium in Daybreak expected to bring economic impacts, growth to local businesses
item.description: South Jordan is home to local businesses and neighbors who say they're starting to feel the effects of increased foot traffic from a new baseball stadium.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified as teenagers, according to media reports. The teens were discovered Jan.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: UVU Ballroom Dance Team wins first place at World Culture Dance Competition
item.description: The Utah Valley University Ballroom Dance Company Tour Team took home the first place award at the World Culture Dance Competition this last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents were anxious Tuesday to return to their flooded homes and assess what's salvageable, even as stubbornly high waters kept some of them waiting even longer.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Utah woman who survived being shot in the head as a teenager now in hospice
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now on hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Murphy, ABC 4

item.title: Arroyo Colorado to be dredged after historic Rio Grande Valley floods
item.description: A Colorado company will design the dredging of a section of a South Texas waterway that flooded during historic recent rains, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commission says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Italy fines Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies $22M for ripping off tourists
item.description: Italy is levying hefty fines against Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies for forcing tourists to pay more to visit the famed historic site.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Chan, KTLA 5

item.title: The trial of an ex-deputy who shot an unarmed Black woman in her home is moved to another city
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The murder trial of a former Illinois sheriff's deputy who fatally shot Sonya Massey, an unarmed black woman, in her Springfield home last summer will be moved out of the capital city to Peoria because of intense publicity, a judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Repeat human smuggler sentenced in South Texas
item.description: A South Texas man who was arrested four times in the past year on human smuggling charges has been sentenced to prison, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
